Title: Use of synthetic biology tools to optimize the production of active nitrogenase Fe protein in chloroplasts of tobacco leaf cells

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast stroma of leaf mesophyll cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for NifH solubility and maturation in chloroplasts; enables accumulation of soluble active NifH.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with nifH; confocal microscopy; chloroplast fractionation; co-purification with NifH; acetylene reduction assay of NifH purified from co-expression lines
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue collected at end of dark period on day 3 after agroinfiltration
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without nifM, NifH forms insoluble puncta/aggregates associated with chloroplast vesicle-like bodies and lacks functionality
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of nifM converted NifH from punctate/membrane-associated accumulation to diffuse soluble stroma localization. 2. NifM was necessary for correct folding/solubility of chloroplast-targeted NifH. 3. NifM co-eluted with purified NifH, supporting direct involvement in maturation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential accessory factor that must be stacked with nifH in plastid engineering strategies for plant nitrogen fixation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank synthetic/optimized gene sequence deposition: MN920692-MN920699

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast stroma of leaf mesophyll cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Fe-S cluster assembly/transfer leading to functional NifH; purified chloroplast NifU is active in apo-NifH reconstitution assays.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NifU Fe-S cluster scaffold protein with Nfu-like C-terminal domain
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression with nifH/nifM/nifS; purification from chloroplasts; iron content measurement; apo-NifH reconstitution assay; acetylene reduction assay
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter or enhanced CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue collected at end of dark period; anaerobic purification and activity assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Protein undergoes partial C-terminal truncation/degradation in plant chloroplasts, likely lowering full activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NifU and NifS were required for detectable NifH activity in chloroplasts. 2. Purified chloroplast NifU carried iron and transferred Fe-S clusters to apo-NifH. 3. NifS co-expression increased NifU iron loading and NifU co-eluted with NifH, consistent with cluster transfer.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Must be included with nifH and nifS for successful plastid nitrogenase engineering; stabilization of full-length NifU may improve performance.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank synthetic/optimized gene sequence deposition: MN920692-MN920699

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast stroma of leaf mesophyll cells
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required together with NifU for Fe-S cluster maturation enabling active NifH accumulation in chloroplasts.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression studies; chloroplast fractionation; NifU co-purification; acetylene reduction assay through effect on NifH activity
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter or enhanced CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Leaf tissue collected at end of dark period; anaerobic handling for protein activity assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No specific tradeoff reported; omission abolishes detectable NifH activity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Co-expression of nifS with nifU supported NifH activation in chloroplasts. 2. NifS co-purified with NifU, supporting interaction in planta. 3. Presence of NifS increased iron loading of NifU, consistent with sulfur donation for Fe-S cluster assembly.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential accessory gene to stack with nifU and nifH for functional plastid nitrogenase assembly.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank synthetic/optimized gene sequence deposition: MN920692-MN920699

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Electron donor component of nitrogenase for biological nitrogen fixation; supports reduction of substrates such as acetylene and physiologically N2
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: chloroplast stroma of leaf mesophyll cells
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nitrogenase assembly / biological nitrogen fixation machinery
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: ATP-dependent electron transfer from NifH to the nitrogenase MoFe protein during substrate reduction; in assay supports reduction of acetylene to ethylene by MoFe protein
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Nitrogenase MoFe protein; ATP; reducing equivalents
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Reduced MoFe protein enabling substrate reduction
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Core catalytic electron-donor component of nitrogenase complex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Synthetic redesign plus chloroplast targeting and co-expression with nifM, nifU and nifS enabled accumulation of soluble, active NifH in tobacco chloroplasts.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key functional component of nitrogenase Fe protein; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Synthetic gene expression comparison; chloroplast targeting screen; confocal microscopy; chloroplast fractionation; affinity purification; Edman sequencing; acetylene reduction assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in agroinfiltrated leaves; highest accumulation at end of dark period on day 3
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark-period tissue harvest; chloroplast localization; anaerobic purification; activity measured with Azotobacter MoFe protein
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast stroma; membrane-associated/vesicular when expressed without nifM
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activity only reached up to ~16% of A. vinelandii NifH, likely due to incomplete maturation and/or oxygen damage to [4Fe-4S] cluster
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Foundational catalytic component for engineering nitrogen fixation in plastids; requires co-expression with maturation factors and oxygen-protection strategies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tobacco-optimized synthetic nifH increased NifH accumulation ~170-fold relative to yeast-optimized sequence. 2. Minimal AtRCA transit peptide efficiently targeted NifH to chloroplasts. 3. Active NifH was purified from tobacco chloroplasts, but only when nifM, nifU and nifS were co-expressed.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although the terminal output is an active protein rather than a classic nutrient metabolite, this is the primary experimentally validated catalytic core gene in the study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank synthetic/optimized gene sequence deposition: MN920692-MN920699
